subject: SCIENCE
title: Animals Around Us
objective: Relate familiar animals to suitable habitats without disturbing wildlife.
introduction: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Animals Around Us”.
A key word used in the lesson “Animals Around Us”.
A key word used in the lesson “Animals Around Us”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Every animal fits one habitat only

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Why does an animal need a habitat?

options: It gives the animal a school grade.
It changes every animal into a pet.

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. It gives the animal a school grade. and It changes every animal into a pet. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Animals Around Us”?

options.text: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.
Every animal fits one habitat only

items.explanation: The correct response is “Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

options.text: Every animal fits one habitat only

items.explanation: The correct response is “fish—water habitat” because it follows the lesson explanation: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: Every animal fits one habitat only

items.explanation: The correct response is “Every animal fits one habitat only” because it follows the lesson explanation: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

items.prompt: Why does an animal need a habitat?

options.text: It gives the animal a school grade.
It changes every animal into a pet.

items.explanation: The correct response is “It provides needed resources.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Every animal fits one habitat only

items.explanation: The correct response is “frog—moist habitat” because it follows the lesson explanation: Habitats provide resources such as food, water, shelter, and space.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Animals Around Us; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Animals Around Us.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
